expenseLibrary.name: Professional Software 🛠️
expenseLibrary.explanation: Success! Subscription-based software (like Adobe or Microsoft 365) is a standard business deduction. ✨
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: Accounting Fact: These are 'Period Costs'—they are recognized in the period they are paid! 🗓️

expenseLibrary.name: Client Business Meal 🍱
expenseLibrary.explanation: Processed! Business meals are generally 50% deductible if a business discussion took place. 🤝
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: Tax Law Tip: Always keep a digital record of the receipt and the business purpose of the meeting! 🧾

expenseLibrary.name: Commute to Rutgers Lab 🚗
expenseLibrary.explanation: Audit Alert! Commuting between your home and your regular workplace is a personal expense, not a deduction. 🛑
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: Managerial Logic: Only travel between two different work sites in the same day is typically deductible. 📉

expenseLibrary.name: New MacBook Pro 💻
expenseLibrary.explanation: Detected! Large hardware purchases are Capital Assets. They provide value for over a year! 🏗️
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: AIS Logic: This should be capitalized and depreciated over its useful life (usually 5 years per MACRS). ⚖️

expenseLibrary.name: Home Office Rent 🏠
expenseLibrary.explanation: Verified! You can deduct the pro-rata portion of your home used EXCLUSIVELY for business. 📏
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: Internal Control Tip: Be careful! The IRS scrutinizes this deduction heavily. Maintain a floor plan for your records! 🕵️‍♀️

expenseLibrary.name: Gym Membership 🏋️‍♀️
expenseLibrary.explanation: Audit Alert! While health is wealth, the IRS usually considers gym memberships a personal expense. ❌
expenseLibrary.knowledgeTip: Auditor Logic: Personal expenses 'comingled' with business accounts are a major red flag in an audit! 🚩
